fact: February 11 is exactly 42 days into the year - that's 6 weeks of winter already survived!
In the Northern Hemisphere, groundhogs are still fast asleep underground, dreaming through their winter naps that won't end for weeks.
The sun rises about 2 minutes earlier than it did just one week ago - winter is secretly losing its grip on daylight!
Tree sap in cold regions is like frozen slushies right now, waiting for warmer days to start flowing again.
February 11 falls smack in the middle of Aquarius season, when the stars supposedly make people extra weird and creative.
In Australia and other Southern Hemisphere places, this date marks the peak of summer heat - perfect for finding cicadas buzzing like tiny chainsaws.
Snow crystals formed on February 11 are more likely to be perfectly symmetrical because the air is super cold and still.
Owls in northern forests are at their chattiest right now, hooting love songs to find mates before spring arrives.
The moon phase on February 11 changes every year, but when it's full, winter nights become bright enough to read outside!
Polar bears in the Arctic are teaching their cubs to hunt seals right around this time - it's like deadly swimming lessons on ice.
February 11 is exactly 38 days before the spring equinox, when day and night become perfectly equal everywhere on Earth.
In places like Canada, the air is so cold that your breath can freeze into tiny ice crystals that tinkle when they hit the ground.
